cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about the process of fitting a working telephone booth in the story. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to go back to the story and find the relevant part. The story mentions that "It was a year ago that the phone line had been installed, six months later men from the public works department had come to put up the phone booth." So the phone line was installed a year ago, and then six months after that, the booth was put up. That would make it 1.5 years total for the process up to that point. But the question is about fitting a working telephone booth. Wait, the story also says that the telephone man is there now, and he mentions that the minister will come next month to inaugurate it. So even though the booth was physically put up six months ago, it's not yet working? Because the inauguration is needed for it to be functional. So the actual process of getting it working includes the installation of the line, the booth, and then the inauguration. The phone line was installed a year ago, the booth six months after that (so 1.5 years ago from the current time in the story?), but the inauguration is still next month. So the total time taken to fit a working booth would be from the initial installation to the inauguration, which is more than a year. But the options are about what the question is asking.
